title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: Note: vignette code evaluated package checks requires running Sirius instance. run interactively, set knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = TRUE) ensure Sirius 6.3 installed running. vignette demonstrates basic workflow importing detected chromatographic peaks XcmsExperiment object Sirius. runs Sirius’s main tools: formula identification, structure database search, compound class prediction, spectral library matching, de novo structure prediction, finally retrieves results. foundational example cover possible parameters Sirius tool. detailed parameter information, consult run() function documentation. information can found Sirius documentation online. vignette focuses chromatographic peaks detected xcms, similar workflow applies features (grouped chromatographic peaks). vignette features pending availability public data, steps data preparation differ slightly can adapted without issue. IMPORTANT: work progress. Feedback highly valued, especially regarding enhancements additions simplify workflow. input user essential.

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: , apply pre-optimized parameters processing example dataset.
code: dda_file <- MsDataHub::PestMix1_DDA.mzML() dda_data <- readMsExperiment(dda_file) spectra(dda_data) <- setBackend(spectra(dda_data), MsBackendMemory()) dda_data <- filterRt(dda_data, rt = c(230, 610))  dda_data |>     spectra() |>     msLevel() |>     table()  prec_int <- estimatePrecursorIntensity(spectra(dda_data)) cwp <- CentWaveParam(snthresh = 5, noise = 100, ppm = 10,                      peakwidth = c(3, 30)) dda_data <- findChromPeaks(dda_data, param = cwp, msLevel = 1L)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: MS2 MS1 spectra corresponding identified chromatographic peaks extracted. feature imported Sirius can one MS1 spectrum. However MS2 can also loaded wiht MS1 wanted.
code: # Extract MS1 and MS2 spectra linked to chromatographic peaks ms2 <- chromPeakSpectra(dda_data,                          expandMz = 0.01, expandRt = 3,                         msLevel = 2L) low_int <- function(x, ...) x > max(x, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.05 ms2 <- filterIntensity(ms2, intensity = low_int) ms2 <- ms2[lengths(ms2) > 1] # cp <- unique(spectraData(ms2)$chrom_peak_id) # ms2 <- ms2[spectraData(ms2)$chrom_peak_id %in% cp]  ms1 <- chromPeakSpectra(dda_data,                           expandMz = 0.01, expandRt = 3,                         msLevel = 1L, method = "closest_rt")  ms1 <- applyProcessing(ms1) ms2 <- applyProcessing(ms2) ms1_ms2 <- concatenateSpectra(ms1, ms2)

what: Open Sirius and project set up
title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: Sirius application initialized via API, requiring project ID. project exists, opened; otherwise, new project created. srs object acts connection Sirius holds project details. Properly shut connection shutdown(srs) completing work. srs variable needed task necessitate communicate application. can learn object class running ?Sirius console. precise path parameter, default Sirius try save project sirius_projects folder user directory. Note folder created automatically. want save somewhere else can specify path = parameter. user wants open perform computations different project can use utility function : can find utility functions package running ?utils console. NOTE idea utility functions implemented hesitate ask.
code: # Initialize Sirius connection srs <- Sirius(projectId = "test_lcmsms") #add `path = "your/directory"` if needed.  srs srs <- openProject(srs, projectId = "test_lcmsms2", path = getwd()) # `path =` also available here srs

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: Preprocessed xcms data imported Sirius, summary data.frame returned feature information. information can also retrieved using utility function featuresInfo(). Notes: also discussed data.frame stored direction srs object running import() automatically create mapping data.frame xcms feature ID Sirius feature ID. stored srs object, featureMap slot. can used future user never need interact Sirius IDs. example extract features ID, utility function featuresId() quickly extract available ID either sirius xcms.
code: ## load back our original project srs <- openProject(srs, "test_lcmsms")  ## Import data into Sirius srs <- import(sirius = srs,               spectra = ms1_ms2,                ms_column_name = "chrom_peak_id",               deleteExistingFeatures = TRUE)  ## See information about the features featuresInfo(srs) |> head() fts_id <- featuresId(srs, type = "sirius")

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.

Whether structure prediction spectral library matching, users can upload custom databases Sirius. vignette, demonstrate test spectral library matching creating loading custom database Sirius. process can also completed easily via Sirius graphical user interface (GUI). prefer interactive approach, can use openGUI(srs) command open Sirius app manage database directly. example, download MassBank library GNPS, needs loaded Sirius generate .sirius.db file. download current directory can precise want save using location = parameter. NOTE: takes quite , change smaller database later. database created loaded, can verify successful import running following command: Find handle databases Sirius typing ?siriusDBs console.
code: ## Download the MassBank EU library download.file("https://external.gnps2.org/gnpslibrary/MASSBANKEU.mgf",               destfile = "MASSBANKEU.mgf") createDB(srs, databaseId = "massbankeuCustom", files = "MASSBANKEU.mgf") listDBs(srs)

what: Submit job to Sirius - For structure DB search
title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: Annotation prediction begin data import. run() function accepts parameters Sirius tool, formula identification, structure database search, compound class prediction. Parameters can also specify adducts custom databases. Detailed documentation parameters available run() function’s help file. wait parameter ensures function waits job completion proceeding. set FALSE, job ID returned, user must check status using jobInfo().
code: ## Start computation job_id <- run(srs,               fallbackAdducts = c("[M + H]+", "[M + Na]+", "[M + K]+"),               spectraSearchParams = spectraMatchingParam(                   spectraSearchDBs = c("BIO", "test")               ),               formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(numberOfCandidates = 5,                                                instrument = "QTOF",                                 numberOfCandidatesPerIonization = 2,                                 massAccuracyMS2ppm = 10,                                 filterByIsotopePattern = FALSE,                                 isotopeMs2Settings = c("SCORE"),                                 performDeNovoBelowMz = 600,                                  minPeaksToInjectSpecLibMatch = 3),               predictParams = predictParam(),               structureDbSearchParams = structureDbSearchParam(                   structureSearchDbs = c("BIO", "test")               ),               recompute = TRUE,               wait = TRUE )  srs ## Get more info for the job jobInfo(srs, job_id) |> cat()

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: obtain summary results, including top formulas, structures, compound class predictions, use following code. summary table provides quick overview evaluate whether results align expectations. However, recommend relying table -detailed analysis. Instead, use functions described later vignette explore results greater depth. important aspect summary table confidence-related columns, provide insight reliability predictions.
code: summarytb <- summary(sirius = srs, result.type = "structure") head(summarytb)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: detailed results, results() function can used result.type parameter set "formulaId", "structureDb", "compoundClass", "deNovo". Note results linked predicted formula. parameters topFormula topStructure allow users specify many formulas structures included output. results can returned either list data.frame, based return.type parameter. Note: Suggestions renaming results() function feedback implementation welcome. aim adapt based user needs.
code: results(srs,         return.type = "data.frame",         result.type = "formulaId",        topFormula = 5)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: following example shows top two structure annotations top five formulas feature. can provide insightful view structural predictions. visual exploration results, can open Sirius GUI commands :
code: finalstructredb <- results(srs,                             return.type = "data.frame",                             result.type = "structureDb",                            topFormula = 5,                            topStructure = 2)  head(finalstructredb) # openGUI(srs) # closeGUI(srs)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: retrieve compound class predictions, use following code. example showing compound annotations confidence scores 50% top two formulas feature.
code: finalcomp <- results(srs,                       return.type = "data.frame",                       result.type = "compoundClass",                      topFormula = 2) head(finalcomp)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: following code gives summary best matches: detailed results, use following code:
code: summaryspectra <- summary(srs, result.type = "spectralDbMatch") head(summaryspectra) full_spectral <- results(srs,                           return.type = "data.frame",                           result.type = "spectralDbMatch",                          topSpectralMatches = 2) head(full_spectral)

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: show get fragmentation tree top2 formula feautres. quite inefficient moment limit little number feature. improve .
code: resulttree <- results(srs,                        features = featuresId(srs)[1:5],                       return.type = "list",                        result.type = "fragTree",                       topFormula = 4,                       )  head(resulttree)

what: Submit job to Sirius - For De Novo structure annotation.
title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: De novo structure annotation computationally intensive recommended specific features.
code: # Determine features that do not have/have poor structure prediction fts_denovo <-summarytb$alignedFeatureId[which(     summarytb$confidenceApproxMatch < 0.3 |         summarytb$confidenceApproxMatch %in% c("NA", "-Infinity"))] # Compute with zodiac and denovo job_id <- run(srs,     msNovelistParams = deNovoStructureParam(numberOfCandidateToPredict = 5),     alignedFeaturesIds = fts_denovo,     recompute = FALSE,      wait = TRUE )   ## Get info for the job jobInfo(srs, job_id) |> cat()

title: Predict formula and structure of chromatographic peaks from an XcmsExperiment object Sirius through the RuSirius package.
text: full results.
code: summraryDeNovo <- summary(srs, result.type = "deNovo") head(summraryDeNovo) full_de_novo <- results(srs,                          return.type = "data.frame",                          result.type = "deNovo",                         topFormula = 5)  head(full_de_novo)

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Note: vignette code evaluated package checks requires running Sirius instance. Sirius can search custom databases addition built-databases (BIO, PubChem, etc.). useful : list suspect compounds specific study custom spectral library (e.g., MassBank) Target compounds want prioritize search vignette demonstrates create use custom databases, shows impact structure identification results.

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: srs <- Sirius()  # List all searchable databases dbs <- listDbs(srs) dbs[, c("databaseId", "displayName")]

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: # Get details about a specific database infoDb(srs, databaseId = "BIO")

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Custom databases can created files containing compound information. Supported formats include .tsv, .csv, .mgf files structure information.

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: file contain columns compound name, SMILES (InChI), optionally molecular formula.
code: # Create database from a TSV file createDb(srs,          databaseId = "my_suspects",          files = "path/to/suspects.tsv",          location = getwd())  # Verify it was created listDbs(srs)

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Spectral libraries MGF format can also imported. example MGF file included package:
code: # Path to example MassBank MGF file mgf_file <- system.file("vignettes", "MASSBANKEU.mgf", package = "RuSirius")  createDb(srs,          databaseId = "massbank_custom",          files = mgf_file,          location = getwd())

what: Comparing Results: Default vs Custom Database
title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Let’s demonstrate using custom database affects structure identification.

previous_headings: Comparing Results: Default vs Custom Database
title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: # Load example data dda_file <- MsDataHub::PestMix1_DDA.mzML() sp <- Spectra(dda_file) sp <- setBackend(sp, MsBackendMemory()) sp <- filterEmptySpectra(sp)  # Group spectra idxs <- fragmentGroupIndex(sp) sp$Msn_idx <- idxs  # Create project and import srs <- Sirius(projectId = "db_comparison", path = getwd()) sp_subset <- sp[sp$Msn_idx %in% c(421, 707)] srs <- import(srs, spectra = sp_subset, ms_column_name = "Msn_idx")

previous_headings: Comparing Results: Default vs Custom Database
title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: # Run structure search with BIO database only run(srs,     formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(numberOfCandidates = 5),     predictParams = predictParam(),     structureDbSearchParams = structureDbSearchParam(         structureSearchDbs = c("BIO")     ),     recompute = TRUE,     wait = TRUE)  # Get results results_bio <- summary(srs, result.type = "structure") results_bio[, c("alignedFeatureId", "molecularFormula",                  "structureName", "confidenceExactMatch")]

previous_headings: Comparing Results: Default vs Custom Database
title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: # Now include custom database in search run(srs,     formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(numberOfCandidates = 5),     predictParams = predictParam(),     structureDbSearchParams = structureDbSearchParam(         structureSearchDbs = c("BIO", "massbank_custom")     ),     recompute = TRUE,     wait = TRUE)  # Get results with custom DB results_custom <- summary(srs, result.type = "structure") results_custom[, c("alignedFeatureId", "molecularFormula",                     "structureName", "confidenceExactMatch")]

previous_headings: Comparing Results: Default vs Custom Database
title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Including relevant custom databases can improve identification confidence compounds well-represented custom database.
code: # Compare confidence scores comparison <- merge(     results_bio[, c("alignedFeatureId", "confidenceExactMatch")],     results_custom[, c("alignedFeatureId", "confidenceExactMatch")],     by = "alignedFeatureId",     suffixes = c("_bio", "_custom") ) comparison

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
code: # Remove a custom database when no longer needed removeDb(srs, databaseId = "massbank_custom")  # Verify removal listDbs(srs)

title: Using Custom Databases in Sirius
text: Targeted databases: Create focused databases compounds relevant study rather large generic databases. Quality quantity: Ensure custom database accurate structure information (SMILES/InChI). Combine strategically: Use custom databases alongside BIO best coverage - BIO general metabolites, custom specific targets. Spectral libraries: available, spectral libraries (MGF) provide additional matching power spectral similarity.

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: RuSirius provides R interface Sirius mass spectrometry software metabolite identification. vignette covers basics connecting Sirius managing projects. Note: vignette code evaluated package checks. run interactively, ensure Sirius 6.3 installed running.

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: using RuSirius, need: Sirius 6.3 installed system. Download official releases page. Sirius account (free registration bright-giant.com)

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: Sirius() function creates connection Sirius application. Sirius running, attempt start automatically. credentials, can log connection:
code: # Basic connection - Sirius will start if not running srs <- Sirius() srs srs <- Sirius(     username = "your_email@example.com",     password = "your_password" )

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: can verify connection valid:
code: # Check if connected checkConnection(srs)  # View connection details srs

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: Sirius organizes data projects. can create new projects open existing ones.

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: project file saved my_analysis.sirius specified path.
code: # Create a new project (or open if it exists) srs <- Sirius(     projectId = "my_analysis",     path = getwd()   )  # Or open a different project later srs <- openProject(srs, projectId = "another_project", path = getwd())

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
code: # Get project details projectInfo(srs)  # List all open projects listOpenProjects(srs)

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: importing data (see “Importing Spectra” vignette), can manage features:
code: # List feature IDs featuresId(srs)  # Get feature information featuresInfo(srs)  # Get the mapping between your IDs and Sirius IDs srs@featureMap

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: didn’t provide credentials connection time:
code: srs <- logIn(srs, username = "your_email@example.com", password = "your_password")

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: can open Sirius graphical interface visual exploration:
code: # Open the GUI openGUI(srs)  # Close the GUI when done closeGUI(srs)

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: Always properly close connection finished:
code: # Close project and shutdown shutdown(srs)  # Or just close the project without shutting down Sirius shutdown(srs, closeProject = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: RuSirius provides several utility functions. See ?utils full list: checkConnection() - Verify connection status logIn() - Log Sirius openProject() / listOpenProjects() - Project management projectInfo() - Get project details featuresId() / featuresInfo() - Feature management deleteFeatures() - Remove features mapFeatures() - Get ID mappings openGUI() / closeGUI() - GUI control shutdown() - Clean shutdown

title: Getting Started with RuSirius
text: See “Importing Spectra Sirius” vignette importing MS data See ?run running Sirius computations (formula ID, structure search, etc.) See ?results retrieving annotation results

title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: Note: vignette code evaluated package checks requires running Sirius instance. run interactively, set knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = TRUE) ensure Sirius 6.3 installed running. vignette demonstrates basic workflow importing MS data Spectra object object Sirius. runs Sirius’s main tools: formula identification, structure database search, compound class prediction, spectral library matching, de novo structure prediction, finally retrieves results. foundational example cover possible parameters Sirius tool. detailed parameter information, consult run() function documentation. information can found Sirius documentation online. IMPORTANT: work progress. Feedback highly valued, especially regarding enhancements additions simplify workflow. input user essential.

title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: load data Spectra Object: import Spectra data Sirius, must preprocessed. multiple MS levels present, need group appropriately. use fragmentGroupIndex() function assign index spectrum. MS2 spectra belong MS1 spectrum share index.
code: dda_file <- MsDataHub::PestMix1_DDA.mzML() sp <- Spectra(dda_file) sp <- setBackend(sp, MsBackendMemory()) sp <- filterEmptySpectra(sp) sp |>     msLevel() |>     table()  idxs <- fragmentGroupIndex(sp) sp$Msn_idx <- idxs

what: Open Sirius and project set up
title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: Sirius application initialized via API, requiring project ID. project exists, opened; otherwise, new project created. srs object acts connection Sirius holds project details. Properly shut connection shutdown(srs) completing work. srs variable needed task necessitate communicate application. can learn object class running ?Sirius console. precise path parameter, default Sirius try save project sirius_projects folder user directory. Note folder created automatically. want save somewhere else can specify path = parameter. import entire Spectra object, demonstration purposes, use selected examples. , import two MS1-MS2 pairs one MS1 spectrum . ’s also possible import MS2 spectra. importing, ms_column_name parameter defines column contains index groups spectra.
code: srs <- Sirius(projectId = "test_spectra", path = getwd()) sp_subset <- sp[sp$Msn_idx %in% c(421, 707, 895)]  srs <- import(sirius = srs,               spectra = sp_subset,               ms_column_name = "Msn_idx",               deleteExistingFeatures = TRUE)  ## See information about the features featuresInfo(srs)

title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: workflows, MS2 (MS2 MS3) spectra available — example, working spectral libraries, MGF files, data acquired without recording MS1 scans. SIRIUS API fully supports importing features without MS1 data. MS1 spectra present ms_column_name provided, import() automatically groups MSn spectra acquisition order: within file (dataOrigin), new group starts whenever new MS2 precursorMz encountered, subsequent higher-level scans (MS3+) assigned group preceding MS2. MSn spectra already grouping column (e.g., feature detection tool), can still pass via ms_column_name usual.
code: ## Example: importing MS2-only spectra ## Assume sp_ms2 is a Spectra object containing only MS2 (and optionally MS3) ## spectra, with no MS1 data. sp_ms2 <- filterMsLevel(sp, msLevel = 2L) sp_ms2 <- sp_ms2[1:10]  # Just an example subset of MS2 spectra ## No need for ms_column_name — the function auto-groups by acquisition order. srs <- import(sirius = srs,               spectra = sp_ms2,               deleteExistingFeatures = TRUE)  featuresInfo(srs)

what: Submit job to Sirius - For structure DB search
title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: data imported, annotation prediction can begin. run() function accepts parameters Sirius tool, formula identification, structure database search, compound class prediction.
code: ## Start computation run(srs,     fallbackAdducts = c("[M + H]+", "[M + Na]+"),     formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(numberOfCandidates = 10,                                        instrument = "QTOF",                         numberOfCandidatesPerIonization = 3,                         massAccuracyMS2ppm = 10,                         filterByIsotopePattern = FALSE,                         isotopeMs2Settings = c("SCORE"),                         performDeNovoBelowMz = 600,                         minPeaksToInjectSpecLibMatch = 3),     predictParams = predictParam(),      structureDbSearchParams = structureDbSearchParam(           structureSearchDbs = c("BIO")       ),     recompute = TRUE,     wait = TRUE     )  ## could test featureInfo vs featureId info <- featuresInfo(srs)

title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: get summary results—including top formulas, structures, compound class predictions—use following: summary table offers quick overview checking whether predictions meet expectations. However, recommend relying solely -depth analysis. Instead, use detailed functions provided later vignette. Key columns include confidence scores help assess result reliability.
code: summarytb <- summary(sirius = srs, result.type = "structure")

title: Importing Spectra into Sirius
text: Interestingly, first feature, results remain consistent, second—originally lower confidence—predictions now differ. visual exploration results, can open Sirius GUI: can look retrieving results ?results documentation. vignette.
code: # Compute with zodiac and denovo run(srs,     msNovelistParams = deNovoStructureParam(numberOfCandidateToPredict = 5),     recompute = FALSE,     wait = TRUE )  summraryDeNovo <- summary(srs, result.type = "deNovo") shutdown(srs)  # openGUI(srs) # closeGUI(srs)

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: Note: vignette code evaluated package checks requires running Sirius instance completed computations. running Sirius computations (see “Importing Spectra” vignette), can retrieve various types results. RuSirius provides two main functions: summary() - Quick overview top results per feature results() - Detailed results multiple candidates

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: First, connect existing Sirius project completed computations:
code: srs <- Sirius(projectId = "my_analysis", path = getwd())

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: summary() function provides top annotation feature. useful quick overview results.

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: Key columns include confidenceApproxMatch (overall confidence feature) confidenceExactMatch (confidence top hit).
code: # Top formula candidates summary_formula <- summary(srs, result.type = "formulaId") head(summary_formula)

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Top structure hits summary_structure <- summary(srs, result.type = "structure")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Top MSNovelist predictions summary_denovo <- summary(srs, result.type = "deNovo")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Best spectral matches summary_spectral <- summary(srs, result.type = "spectralDbMatch")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: results() function returns multiple candidates per feature, giving options explore.

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Get top 5 formula candidates per feature formulas <- results(srs,                      result.type = "formulaId",                     topFormula = 5,                     return.type = "data.frame") head(formulas)

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: Key columns: inchiKey, smiles, structureName, csiScore.
code: # Get structure candidates for each formula structures <- results(srs,                       result.type = "structureDb",                       topFormula = 3,                       topStructure = 5,                       return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: Returns ClassyFire NPC classifications confidence scores.
code: # Get predicted compound classes classes <- results(srs,                    result.type = "compoundClass",                    topFormula = 1,                    return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Get de novo predicted structures denovo <- results(srs,                   result.type = "deNovo",                   topFormula = 1,                   topStructure = 5,                   return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Get spectral library matches spectral <- results(srs,                     result.type = "spectralDbMatch",                     topSpectralMatches = 5,                     return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
code: # Get fragmentation tree data fragtrees <- results(srs,                      result.type = "fragTree",                      topFormula = 1,                      return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: can retrieve results specific features :
code: # Get feature IDs feature_ids <- featuresId(srs)  # Get results for first two features only subset_results <- results(srs,                           features = feature_ids[1:2],                           result.type = "structureDb",                           return.type = "data.frame")

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: Results can returned data.frame (default) list:
code: # As list - useful for per-feature processing results_list <- results(srs,                         result.type = "formulaId",                         return.type = "list")  # Access results for a specific feature results_list[["feature_id_here"]]

title: Retrieving Results from Sirius
text: imported data xcms, results include original xcms feature IDs xcms_fts column easy mapping back original data.
code: # Get the feature mapping mapFeatures(srs)

what: R Implementation of the Sirius software
title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: IMPORTANT: ACTIVELY DEVELOPED. RuSirius R package provides interface Sirius software, enabling seamless integration xcms preprocessing results Sirius. built upon RSirius, REST API library R programming language, can find .

what: Option 1: Install Sirius and R-api via Conda
title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: can install Sirius software R API using conda following command: install RuSirius ’s dependencies :
code: conda install conda-forge::r-sirius-ms library(remotes) BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/RuSirius")

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: Alternatively, can manually install Sirius RuSirius: Download Sirius software official releases page. RuSirius dependent Sirius 6.3, please ensure right version downloaded. Install RuSirius ’s dependencies (includes R-api) running following code R:
code: library(remotes) BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/RuSirius")

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: RuSirius comes five vignettes help get started: Getting Started RuSirius vignette covers basics connecting Sirius managing projects. Importing Spectra Sirius vignette shows basic example importing spectra data Sirius. Retrieving Results Sirius vignette explains retrieve interpret different result types. Using Custom Databases vignette demonstrates create use custom databases structure search. Importing Chromatographic Peaks xcms vignette demonstrates import chromatographic peaks xcms use Sirius.

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: ’re looking testers help refine enhance functionality package, focusing users need . feedback crucial ensuring package meets user expectations operates smoothly.

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: Ease Installation Start testing installation process. Let us know encounter issues, especially package dependencies. Exploring Basic Functionalities Run first two vignettes familiarize connecting Sirius importing spectra data. give solid foundation understanding package works. Adapting Dataset ’re working custom datasets want import chromatographic peaks xcms, try third vignette. provides necessary code steps advanced use cases.

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: current implementation early stages, focusing basic functionality. input essential making integration xcms Sirius seamless user-friendly. Whether suggestions, encounter issues, need additional features, don’t hesitate reach . insights directly shape future updates.

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: early stage integrating Sirius RforMassSpectrometry, ’s ongoing development enhance implementation.

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: GHA R command checks: failing/incomplete vignettes run systematically (requires Sirius login). Docker image: Dockerfile available set environment, vignettes run automatically require Sirius authentication. See Docker section usage instructions. Importing features can time-consuming. speed testing, import features first limit process one MS1 spectrum per feature. details provided vignettes. encounter issues, please let us know can improve package!

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: Docker image provided based bioconductor/bioconductor_docker:RELEASE_3_22. includes RuSirius, dependencies, Sirius 6.3 software (installed via conda).

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
code: docker build -t rformassspectrometry/rusirius .

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: open RStudio Server http://localhost:8787 (user: rstudio, password: set ).
code: docker run -e PASSWORD=yourpassword -p 8787:8787 rformassspectrometry/rusirius

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: Vignettes require active Sirius session. starting container, log R console: Note Sirius requires valid account — can register free https://bright-giant.com.
code: library(RuSirius) srs <- Sirius(projectId = "my_project",               username = "your_email",               password = "your_password")

title: R Implementation of the Sirius software
text: Many many thanks Markus Fleischauer work Sirius SDKs, many Jonas Emmert made R-API usable. Marcus Ludwig help support implementing RuSirius.

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
text: function set parameter de novo structure annotation using MSNovelist tool. MSNovelist generates molecular structures de novo MS/MS data - without relying database. makes particularly useful analyzing poorly represented analyte classes novel compounds, traditional database searches may fall short. However, intended replace database searches altogether, structural elucidation small molecules MS/MS data remains challenging task, identifying structure without database candidates even difficult.

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
code: deNovoStructureParam(numberOfCandidateToPredict = 10)

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
text: numberOfCandidateToPredict numeric, number structure candidates predicted MsNovelist. Max Value 128. Actual number returned candidate might lower du duplicates created. Default 10

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
text: object class deNovoStructureParam.

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
text: information, see Sirius documentation.

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
text: reference

title: de novo structure annotation — deNovoStructureParam
code: # Example of setting up the parameters for de novo structure annotation param <- deNovoStructureParam(numberOfCandidateToPredict = 10)

title: Identifying molecular formula — formulaIdParam
text: function configures parameters molecular formula annotation Sirius. Molecular formula identification done using isotope pattern analysis MS1 data well fragmentation tree computation MS2 data. score molecular formula candidate combination isotope pattern score fragmentation tree score.

title: Identifying molecular formula — formulaIdParam
code: formulaIdParam(   instrument = c("QTOF", "ORBITRAP", "FTICR"),   numberOfCandidates = 10,   numberOfCandidatesPerIonization = 1,   massAccuracyMS2ppm = 10,   isotopeMs2Settings = c("IGNORE", "FILTER", "SCORE"),   filterByIsotopePattern = TRUE,   enforceElGordoFormula = TRUE,   performBottomUpSearch = TRUE,   performDeNovoBelowMz = 400,   formulaSearchDBs = character(0),   applyFormulaConstraintsToDBAndBottomUpSearch = FALSE,   enforcedFormulaConstraints = c("H", "C", "N", "O", "P"),   fallbackFormulaConstraints = c("S"),   detectableElements = c("B", "S", "Cl", "Se", "Br"),   ilpTimeout = FALSE,   numberOfSecondsPerDecomposition = 0,   numberOfSecondsPerInstance = 0,   useHeuristic = TRUE,   useHeuristicAboveMz = 300,   useOnlyHeuristicAboveMz = 650,   injectSpecLibMatchFormulas = TRUE,   minScoreToInjectSpecLibMatch = 0.7,   minPeaksToInjectSpecLibMatch = 6 )

title: Identifying molecular formula — formulaIdParam
text: instrument character(1) type mass spectrometer used analysis. Options include "QTOF", "ORBITRAP", "FTICR". choice mainly affects allowed mass deviation. unsure instrument, use default value "QTOF". numberOfCandidates integer(1) number formula candidates keep result list. Default 10. numberOfCandidatesPerIonization integer(1) Forces SIRIUS report least number candidates per ionization. massAccuracyMS2ppm numeric(1) maximum allowed mass deviation (parts per million, ppm) molecular formulas. formulas within mass window considered. Default 10. isotopeMs2Settings character(1) Specifies isotope patterns MS/MS handled. Default "IGNORE". Options:   filterByIsotopePattern logical TRUE, filters molecular formulas comparing theoretical isotope patterns measured ones, excluding match. Default TRUE. enforceElGordoFormula logical El Gordo may predict MS/MS spectrum lipid spectrum. enabled, corresponding molecular formula enforeced molecular formula candidate. Default TRUE. performBottomUpSearch logical TRUE, enables molecular formula generation bottom-search. Default TRUE. performDeNovoBelowMz numeric(1) Specifies m/z de novo molecular formula generation enabled. Set 0 disable de novo molecular formula generation. Default 400. formulaSearchDBs list(character) list structure databases (e.g., "CHEBI", "HMDB") molecular formulas extracted reduce search space. Use necessary, de novo formula annotation usually effective. Default character(0). applyFormulaConstraintsToDBAndBottomUpSearch logical TRUE, applies formula (element) constraints database search bottom-search, addition de novo generation. Default FALSE. enforcedFormulaConstraints character Specifies elements always considered auto-detecting formula. Enforced elements always included formula, even compound already assigned specific molecular formula. Default H,C,N,O,P. fallbackFormulaConstraints character Specifies elements used fallback auto-detection fails (e.g., isotope pattern). Default S. detectableElements list(character) Defines elements can added chemical alphabet detected spectrum, isotope patterns. Default c("B", "S", "Cl", "Se", "Br"). ilpTimeout logical timeout settings integer linear programming (ILP) solver. TRUE, include timeout parameters numberOfSecondsPerDecomposition numberOfSecondsPerInstance. numberOfSecondsPerDecomposition numeric numberOfSecondsPerInstance numeric useHeuristic logical TRUE, enables use heuristics molecular formula annotation. enabled, additional thresholds like useHeuristicAboveMz useOnlyHeuristicAboveMz can set. useHeuristicAboveMz numeric m/z threshold heuristic used. Default 300. useOnlyHeuristicAboveMz numeric(1) m/z threshold heuristic used. Default 650. injectSpecLibMatchFormulas logical TRUE, formula candidates matching spectral library entries certain similarity threshold preserved analysis, regardless score filter settings. Default TRUE. minScoreToInjectSpecLibMatch numeric(1) similarity threshold injecting spectral library match formulas. score threshold, formula preserved. Default 0.7. minPeaksToInjectSpecLibMatch integer minimum number matching peaks required inject spectral library match formulas analysis.
code: - `"FILTER"`: Excludes formulas if the theoretical isotope pattern      doesn't match.    - `"SCORE"`: Uses isotope patterns for scoring, useful for clear      MS/MS isotope patterns.    - `"IGNORE"`: Ignores isotope patterns in MS/MS.

title: Import Data into Sirius — import
text: Import Data Sirius

title: Import Data into Sirius — import
code: import(   sirius,   spectra,   ms_column_name = character(),   adducts = character(),   chunkSize = 500,   deleteExistingFeatures = TRUE )

title: Import Data into Sirius — import
text: sirius Sirius. connection Sirius instance loaded project. spectra Spectra object containing MS data import. Can contain MS1 /MS2 spectra. multiple MS levels present, ms_column_name parameter must provided group spectra features. See fragmentGroupIndex() function Spectra package generating appropriate grouping indices. ms_column_name character(1). name column expected spectraData input spectra object. MS1 MS2 spectra index column grouped single feature. parameter must provided object multiple msLevels. column name provided object contains one MS level, MS1 spectrum exported separate feature Sirius. Lastly, support multiple MS2 spectra per feature, one MS1 spectrum per index. Please merge MS1 spectra beforehand necessary. adducts character vector adduct(s) associated features imported. Must either length 1 length number features. length 1 fewer number features, adduct used features. chunkSize numeric(1). Number features process import . Importing can slow, useful working large number spectra. deleteExistingFeatures logical(1). TRUE, existing features deleted importing new ones.

what: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
text: function creates object class predictParam can used predict molecular fingerprints compound identifications using CSI:FingerID CANOPUS. CSI:FIngerID identifies structure molecule predicting molecular fingerprint using fingerprint search molecular structure database. CANOPUS (Dührkop et al.) predicts presense/absense 2500 compound classes. CANOPUS predicts classes based solely MS/MS data without requiring database information. means can identify class even molecular structure class exists molecular structure database.

title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
code: predictParam(useScoreThreshold = TRUE, alwaysPredictHighRefMatches = FALSE)

title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
text: useScoreThreshold logical whether use soft threshold applied compute FingerPrints promising formula candidates. Enabling highly recommended. Default TRUE. alwaysPredictHighRefMatches logical wether predict FingerPrint/Classes&Structure formulas candidates reference spectrum similarity > Sirius.minReferenceMatchScoreToInject matter score threshold rules apply. Default FALSE.

title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
text: information, see Sirius documentation.

title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
text: reference

title: Predicting FingerPrint and compounds Identifications — predictParam
code: # Example of setting up the parameters for the prediction of molecular # fingerprints and compound class param <- predictParam()

title: Fetch Results from Sirius — results
text: Functions retrieve results Sirius project, allowing customization result type, feature selection, return format. Get best match results Sirius project. Choose type results fetch result.type parameter. "structure" "deNovo" also give information formula.

title: Fetch Results from Sirius — results
code: summary(   sirius,   result.type = c("formulaId", "structure", "deNovo", "spectralDbMatch") )  results(   sirius,   features = character(),   result.type = c("formulaId", "structureDb", "compoundClass", "deNovo",     "spectralDbMatch", "fragTree"),   return.type = c("list", "data.frame"),   topFormula = 5,   topStructure = 5,   topSpectralMatches = 5 )

title: Fetch Results from Sirius — results
text: sirius Sirius object. result.type character(1) specifying type results fetch. Options "formulaId", "structureDb", "compoundClass", "deNovo", "spectralDbMatch", "fragTree". Defaults "formulaId". features character() vector specifying feature IDs retrieve results . Defaults available features. return.type character(1) specifying return format. Either "data.frame" (default) "list". topFormula numeric(1) Maximum number formula candidates retrieve per feature. Defaults 5. topStructure numeric(1) Maximum number structure candidates per formula. Defaults 5. topSpectralMatches numeric(1) Maximum number spectral matches per feature. Defaults 5.

title: Fetch Results from Sirius — results
text: data.frame list results, depending return.type. data.frame summary results. Important column ApproximateConfidence column, give score possible identifications feature . exactConfidence column score possible top identification .

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: function configures job submission Sirius. creates object class config can used submit job Sirius, can also saved reused later saveJobConfig()function. example use, see vignette. Depending task want perform, can specify following parameters: spectraMatchingParam: Allows perform matching betweeen spectra input spectral libraries. formulaIdParam: Allows generate molecular formula candidates features. zodiacParam: Allows perform re-ranking formula candidates using Zodiac. advised perform De Novo structure annotation run later. predictParam: Allows perform molecular fingeprint prediction using CSI:FingerID compound classification using CANOPUS. structureDbSearchParam: Allows perform structure annotation based fingerprint identifications. deNovoStructureParam: Allows perform de novo structure generation using MSNovelist tool.

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
code: run(   sirius,   compoundsIds = character(),   alignedFeaturesIds = featuresId(sirius),   fallbackAdducts = c("[M + H]+", "[M - H]-", "[M + Na]+", "[M + K]+"),   enforceAdducts = character(),   detectableAdducts = c("[M + H3N + H]+", "[M - H4O2 + H]+", "[M - H2O - H]-",     "[M - H3N - H]-", "[M + Cl]-", "[2M + K]+", "[M + K]+", "[2M + Cl]-",     "[M + C2H4O2 - H]-", "[M + H]+", "[2M + H]+", "[M - CH3 - H]-", "[M - H]-",     "[M + Na]+", "[M - H2O + H]+"),   spectraSearchParams = NA,   formulaIdParams = NA,   zodiacParams = NA,   predictParams = NA,   structureDbSearchParams = NA,   msNovelistParams = NA,   recompute = FALSE,   configFile = character(),   wait = TRUE )  config(   compoundsIds = character(),   alignedFeaturesIds = character(),   fallbackAdducts = c("[M + H]+", "[M - H]-", "[M + Na]+", "[M + K]+"),   enforceAdducts = character(),   detectableAdducts = c("[M + H3N + H]+", "[M - H4O2 + H]+", "[M - H2O - H]-",     "[M - H3N - H]-", "[M + Cl]-", "[2M + K]+", "[M + K]+", "[2M + Cl]-",     "[M + C2H4O2 - H]-", "[M + H]+", "[2M + H]+", "[M - CH3 - H]-", "[M - H]-",     "[M + Na]+", "[M - H2O + H]+"),   formulaIdParams = formulaIdParam(),   zodiacParams = NA,   predictParams = NA,   structureDbSearchParams = NA,   msNovelistParams = NA,   spectraSearchParams = NA,   recompute = FALSE )

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: sirius Sirius object, connection Sirius server. compoundsIds character vector, ids compounds process. alignedFeaturesIds character vector, ids aligned features process. default, computes . fallbackAdducts character vector, fallback adducts considered auto detection find indication ion mode. enforceAdducts character vector, adducts enforce. always considered. detectableAdducts character vector, detectable adducts considered indication MS1 scan (e.g. correct mass delta). spectraSearchParams object class spectraMatchingParam, containing parameters spectra matching. formulaIdParams object class formulaIdParam, containing parameters molecular formula identification. zodiacParams object class zodiacParam, containing parameters Zodiac re-ranking. predictParams object class predictParam, containing parameters molecular fingerprint prediction compound classification. structureDbSearchParams object class structureDbParam, containing parameters structure annotation. msNovelistParams object class deNovoStructureParam, containing parameters de novo structure generation. recompute logical, whether recompute job , necessary configfile comes saved file. Default FALSE. configFile character, path configuration file use job submission, optional. wait logical, whether wait job finish. Default TRUE

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: job ID submitted job, can inputted jobInfo() function retrieve job information. retrieve results see results documentation.

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: want perform formula identification, can inputing formulaIdParam object. combination, can also input spectraMatchingParam object perform spectral matching subsequently compare results.

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: performe structure annotation, need input formulaIdParam object, well predictParam object perform molecular fingerprint prediction compound classification. results subsequently used perform structure annotation using structureDbSearchParam object.

title: Run job on Sirius. — run
text: perform de novo structure annotation, need input formulaIdParam object, also advised case perform re-ranking using zodiacParam object. molecular fingerprint prediction compound classification can performed using predictParam object. deNovoStructureParam object used perform de novo structure annotation.

what: Functions for Handling Sirius Databases — siriusDbs
title: Functions for Handling Sirius Databases — siriusDbs
text: set functions provides tools managing databases Sirius. include listing available databases, retrieving database information, deleting databases, creating new databases files. List databases searchable Sirius spectral matching. Retrieve detailed information specific database. Delete database Sirius. Create new database Sirius specified files.

title: Functions for Handling Sirius Databases — siriusDbs

title: Spectra database matching — spectraMatchingParam
text: function set parameter matching spectra databases. needs run first. Spectral library matching performed using cosine score squared peak intensities, ignoring precursor peak. Note spectral library matches added annotations CSI:FingerID results influence ranking structure candidates.

title: Spectra database matching — spectraMatchingParam
code: spectraMatchingParam(   spectraSearchDBs = c("BIO", "massbank"),   peakDeviationPpm = 10,   precursorDeviationPpm = 10,   scoring = c("MODIFIED_COSINE", "INTENSITY", "GAUSSIAN") )

title: Spectra database matching — spectraMatchingParam
text: spectraSearchDBs character vector databases search. default c("BIO", "massbank"). values can found running listDatabases(). peakDeviationPpm numeric Maximum allowed mass deviation ppm matching peaks. precursorDeviationPpm numeric Maximum allowed mass deviation ppm matching precursor. specified, value peaks used. scoring character scoring function use. Possible values MODIFIED_COSINE, GAUSSIAN, INTENSITY. default MODIFIED_COSINE.

title: Spectra database matching — spectraMatchingParam
text: object class spectraMatchingParam

title: Spectra database matching — spectraMatchingParam

title: Structure Database Search — structureDbSearchParam
text: function creates object class structureDbSearchParam can used configure structure database search Sirius. object can passed runSirius function perform structure database search. default, SIRIUS searches molecular structures biomolecule structure database. can also search (extremely large) PubChem database custom “suspect databases” provided user.

title: Structure Database Search — structureDbSearchParam
code: structureDbSearchParam(   structureSearchDbs = c("BIO", "massbank"),   tagStructuresWithLipidClass = TRUE,   expansiveSearchConfidenceMode = c("APPROXIMATE", "EXACT", "OFF") )

title: Structure Database Search — structureDbSearchParam
text: structureSearchDbs character vector, specifying id databases search structures. Default c("BIO", "massbank"). expansive search enabled database selection expanded PubChem high confidence hit found selected databases. available databases can found running listDatabases(). tagStructuresWithLipidClass logical, specifying whether tag structures lipid class estimated El Gordo. lipid class available El Gordo predicts MS/MS  lipid spectrum. parameter set FALSE El Gordo still executed e.g. improve fragmentation tree, matching structure candidates tagged match lipid class. expansiveSearchConfidenceMode character(1) , specifying confidence mode expansive search. Possible values "APPROXIMATE","EXACT", "". Defaults "APPROXIMATE".

code: logIn(sirius, username, password)  checkConnection(sirius)  shutdown(sirius, closeProject = TRUE)  openGUI(sirius)  closeGUI(sirius, closeProject = FALSE)  projectInfo(sirius, infoType = c("compatibilityInfo", "sizeInformation"))  listOpenProjects(sirius)  openProject(sirius, projectId, path = character())  featuresId(sirius, type = c("sirius", "xcms"))  featuresInfo(sirius)  deleteFeatures(sirius, featureId = featuresId(sirius))  mapFeatures(sirius)  saveConfig(sirius, config, name)  jobInfo(sirius, jobId = character())  deleteJob(sirius, jobId = character(), all = FALSE)

title: Utility function for RuSirius — utils
text: sirius Sirius object username character(1), username use connection password character(1), password use connection closeProject logical, whether also close project closing GUI. Default FALSE. infoType character vector length 1 2, specifying type information retrieve. Possible values "compatibilityInfo" "sizeInformation". projectId character(1) specifying id project open. can already existing project new one. path character(1) path find existing project create new one.default, porject opened current "." directory. type character vector length 1, specifying type features ID list. Possible values "sirius" "xcms". Default "sirius". featureId character() vector specifying id feature remove. default remove . config configSirius object name character vector length 1, specifying name configuration load. jobId character(1) specifying id job delete. logical, whether delete jobs. Default FALSE.

Sirius object features removed. data.frame features mapping. nothing, save configuration locally. character vector job information. nothing, delete job Sirius.

what: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
title: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
text: function configures parameters re-ranking previously computed molecular formula annotation Sirius. step quite computationally memory demanding, advised perform de novo structure annotation used later ZODIAC uses top X molecular formula candidates molecule SIRIUS build similarity network, uses Bayesian statistics re-rank candidates.

title: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
code: zodiacParam(   consideredCandidatesAt300Mz = 10,   consideredCandidatesAt800Mz = 50,   runInTwoSteps = TRUE,   edgeFilterThreshold = TRUE,   thresholdFilter = 0.95,   minLocalCandidates = 1,   minLocalConnections = 10,   gibbsSamplerParameters = TRUE,   iterations = 20000,   burnInPeriod = 2000,   numberOfMarkovChains = 10 )

title: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
text: consideredCandidatesAt300Mz integer(1) specifying maximum number candidate molecular formulas considered ZODIAC compounds 300 m/z. Default 10. consideredCandidatesAt800Mz integer(1) specifying maximum number candidate molecular formulas considered ZODIAC \ compounds 800 m/z. Default 50. runInTwoSteps logical specifying whether ZODIAC uses 2-step approach. First running 'good quality compounds' , afterwards including remaining compounds. Default TRUE. edgeFilterThreshold logical value specifying whether ZODIAC uses edge filter threshold. Default TRUE. thresholdFilter numeric(1) value specifying threshold filter. Default 0.95. minLocalCandidates numeric(1) value specifying minimum number local candidates. Default 1. minLocalConnections numeric(1) value specifying minimum number local connections. Default 10. gibbsSamplerParameters logical value specifying whether ZODIAC uses Gibbs sampler parameters. Default TRUE. iterations numeric(1) specifying number iterations. Default 20000. burnInPeriod numeric(1) specifying burn-period. Default 2000. numberOfMarkovChains numeric(1) specifying number Markov chains. Default 10.

title: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
text: object class zodiacParam.

title: Configuration fr re-ranking of Molecular Formula Annotation — zodiacParam
code: # Example of setting up the parameter for Zodiac re-ranking param <- zodiacParam(consideredCandidatesAt300Mz = 10,                      consideredCandidatesAt800Mz = 50,                      runInTwoSteps = TRUE,                      edgeFilterThreshold = TRUE,                      thresholdFilter = 0.95,                      minLocalCandidates = 1,                      minLocalConnections = 10,                      gibbsSamplerParameters = TRUE,                      iterations = 20000,                      burnInPeriod = 2000,                      numberOfMarkovChains = 10)

title: Version 0.2
text: Fixed bug importing single MS2 spectrum (MS1) resulted 0 features project, causing run() fail “Either ‘compoundsIds’ ‘alignedFeaturesIds’ must provided.” import() now correctly handles single-level MS2-data grouping spectra via .groupMSnIndex() (previously triggered multi-level MSn data). .createfeatures() now always sets ionMass precursorMz MSn data available. Previously ionMass hardcoded 0, caused Sirius API silently discard features without MS1 data. MS1-features, ionMass remains 0 Sirius can derive MS1 spectrum. Updated RSirius dependency latest release (3.1+sirius6.3.3, commit 2c983e6). new version exposes gui_api proper OpenGui, CloseGui, GetGuis endpoints. Simplified openGUI() closeGUI() use official gui_api endpoints instead manually constructing HTTP requests via .callGuiApi(). Removed internal .callGuiApi() helper (longer needed). Added docker-push.yaml GitHub Actions workflow automated Docker image builds push main.

title: Version 0.2
text: Added support importing MSn-data (MS2, MS2+MS3, etc.) without requiring MS1 spectra. SIRIUS API accepts features MS1 data. Added .groupMSnIndex() helper automatically groups MSn-spectra acquisition order ms_column_name provided MS1 data present. .createfeatures() now includes MS3+ spectra (msLevel >= 2) ms2Spectra field sent API. Fixed bug import() ms_column_name used assigned input Spectra contained single MS level. Added “Importing MS2-MSn-data” section ImportSpectra vignette. Added test-msn-import.R test file 30 expectations covering MSn-grouping, feature creation, API integration.

title: Version 0.2
text: Replaced msdata dependency MsDataHub example data vignettes. Added “Getting Started” vignette covering basic connection project management. Added “Retrieving Results” vignette explaining result types retrieval. Added “Custom Databases” vignette creating using custom databases. Updated Dockerfile Bioconductor 3.22.

title: Version 0.2
text: BREAKING: Updated RSirius dependency commit c35ebbb e4acd1b (API version 3.1, Sirius 6.3.3 support). BREAKING: Package dependency renamed Rsirius RSirius (capital S) match upstream rename. Updated Sirius version requirement 6.1 6.3. Updated import take single Spectra object. See documentation information. Added vignette basic example. Updated use Spectra::fragmentGroupIndex() grouping MS1/MS2 spectra. Added comprehensive test suite testthat parameter classes, helper functions, integration tests.

title: Version 0.1
text: Refactor default path opening/saving projects. now save default current directory path = parameter precised. Can now import directly Spectra object, without process beforehand. Still needs improved, see issue #27

title: Version 0.1
text: Now supports import negative polarity. See #24 Addition critical issue readme file (line 115). Related issue #19. Fix issue show() method.

title: Version 0.1
text: Now can choose delete features already present importing new ones. parameter deleteExistingFeatures = import(). Default TRUE. Addition functionalities create, delete list databases. See #15 Can now import feature set adducts known. See #14 Small fixes show() method Sirius object. See #8 Fix spectral Library matching now can see summary full results using summary() results() function precising result.type = "spectralDbMatch". Added results() fragmentation tree. accessible using result.type = "fragTree". See #18 Added DockerFile, seem start Sirius properly. Work Progress See #17

title: Version 0.1
text: Fix installation Spectra dev version Addition code install devel version xcms readme.md

title: Version 0.1
text: Addition package used vignettes DESCRIPITON. Change vignette builder Rmarkdown. Vignettes now go R command checks.

title: Version 0.1
text: Beginning RuSirius 😎
